static.name: L3Harris Technologies, Inc.
static.description: L3Harris Technologies, Inc. provides mission-critical solutions for government and commercial customers worldwide. It operates through three segments: Space & Mission Systems (SMS), Communications & Spectrum Dominance (CSD), and Missile Solutions (MSL). The SMS segment integrates satellite and payload capabilities, including missile warning and defense, with maritime, air special missions, and other global defense and civil government programs. Its MSL segment unites propulsion, hypersonic, and other advanced missile technologies. The CSD segment provides tactical radios, software, waveforms, satellite terminals, and end-to-end battlefield systems for the U.S. Department of Defense, international, federal, and state agency customers; broadband communications; integrated vision solutions, such as helmet-mounted integrated night vision goggles with image intensifier tubes and weapon-mounted sights, aiming lasers, and range finders; and public safety radios and system applications and equipment. The company was formerly known as Harris Corporation and changed its name to L3Harris Technologies, Inc. in June 2019. L3Harris Technologies, Inc. was founded in 1895 and is headquartered in Melbourne, Florida.

claude.thesis: Tactical radios, satellite payloads, electronic warfare, space systems. Best prime growth but still large-cap.

news.headline: How L3Harris’ Fiber‑Optic Tether Production Ramp Will Impact L3Harris Technologies (LHX) Investors

news.headline: A Look At L3Harris Technologies (LHX) Valuation After Fiber Optic Tether Production Expansion
news.summary: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) is expanding production of its fiber-optic tether systems for military drones and underwater platforms, responding to heightened electronic warfare threats and growing demand for secure communications in situations where radio signals risk disruption.

news.headline: L3Harris Expands Fiber Tether Output As Electronic Warfare Shapes Defense Demand

news.headline: Gina Rinehart Invests $97 Million in US Defense Stocks
news.summary: Australia's richest person expanded Hancock Prospecting's defense holdings while increasing exposure to global mining companies.

news.headline: Can Cognyte's $5M APAC Security Deal Strengthen its Revenue Pipeline?
news.summary: CGNT lands a $5M APAC agency upgrade deal, boosting AI-driven investigative tools as governments ramp digital intelligence spending.

news.headline: How Is Rocket Lab Strengthening Its End-to-End Space Model?
news.summary: RKLB is building an end-to-end space model launch, spacecraft systems and mission ops to diversify revenues and deepen its market position.

news.headline: Cathie Wood Shifts ARK's Defense Stocks; Contracts, Foreign Sales In Focus
news.summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest loads up on defense, air taxi stocks. Navy awards contracts to Lockheed, Northrop. U.S. announces foreign military sales.

news.headline: How Is RTX Expanding Its Software-Defined Radar Capabilities?
news.summary: RTX lands a Naval Research contract to advance software-defined radar, enabling future naval systems to run multi-mission sensing in a crowded spectrum.
